data.title: Danaher’s $10 bln M&A pivot demands faith
data.description:
By Robert Cyran

A post-Covid slump challenged the serial acquirer’s playbook of buying profitable biological product makers. A Masimo deal changes focus to patient monitoring. The switch and the seller’s odd history explain adverse investor reactions. Opportunism doesn’t imply lack of targets.

Full view will be published shortly.

Follow Robert Cyran on Bluesky.

CONTEXT NEWS

Danaher said on February 17 that it had agreed to buy Masimo, a company that makes patient monitoring systems, in a $9.9 billion deal including debt. Danaher will pay $180 for each share of Masimo, a premium of 38% to their closing price on February 13, the last day of trading before the deal was announced.

Citigroup served as financial advisor to Danaher, with Goldman Sachs providing advisory support. Centerview Partners served as advisor to Masimo, with Morgan Stanley also providing advice.

data.title: Bitcoin stays pinned below $70K as BTC’s negative funding rate flashes warning sign
data.description:
Key takeaways:

  • Bitcoin’s futures funding rates briefly turned negative, signaling that bullish traders currently lack the conviction to use leverage.

  • Uncertainty regarding the long-term profitability of artificial intelligence has pushed investors toward gold and US government bonds.

Bitcoin (BTC) failed to reclaim the $70,000 level on Tuesday following a retraction in the S&P 500 futures. Traders are concerned that investments in the artificial intelligence sector could take longer to mature, which pressured shares of Nvidia (NVDA US), Apple (AAPL US), and Google (GOOGL US) on Friday. Bearishness in Bitcoin futures became apparent, leading traders to fear further downside.

The annualized BTC futures funding rate briefly flipped negative on Monday, indicating a lack of demand for leveraged long positions. Under neutral conditions, this indicator typically ranges between 6% and 12%; consequently, a lack of conviction from bulls has been the norm for the past week. The recent dominance of precious metals has also contributed to the disappointment of Bitcoin investors.

Silver and gold emerged as clear winners over the past two months while the stock market entered a consolidation period. Gains in the tech sector have come to a standstill as some analysts argue that valuations have become excessive, while others claim efficiency gains from AI are finally paying off. Regardless of the outcome, investors sought protection in government bonds.

Yields on the 10-year US Treasury declined to their lowest levels since November 2025, signaling that demand for these bonds has increased. This trend does not necessarily reflect higher confidence in the Federal Reserve’s strategy to avoid a recession without fueling inflation. In fact, the US dollar has weakened against a basket of foreign currencies, as reflected in the DXY index.

Dario Amodei, co-founder and CEO of Anthropic, reportedly stated on Friday that revenues from AI investments are unlikely to pay off in the next couple of years. According to Fortune, he warned that spending massive amounts to build data centers quickly could be "ruinous." 

Amodei also noted that delivering $10 trillion of compute by mid-2027 is impossible due to capacity constraints. This uncertainty in the tech sector has pushed investors toward more risk-averse behavior.

Bitcoin options market stabilizes as macroeconomic uncertainty lingers

Demand for neutral-to-bearish strategies using BTC options has stagnated over the past week. The panic following the unexpected crash to $60,200 on Feb. 6 has largely subsided, yet traders are still far from flipping bullish.

Related: Bitcoin accumulation wave puts $80K back in play–Analyst

The BTC options put-to-call ratio at Deribit stood at 0.8x on Monday, indicating balanced demand between put (sell) and call (buy) instruments. This data contrasts sharply with the 1.5x ratio seen last Wednesday, a level typically deemed bearish. While it will likely take a couple of weeks for bulls to regain full confidence, Bitcoin derivatives metrics currently show no signs of panic among market participants.

Traders may have opted to act more cautiously, choosing to take profits after Bitcoin flirted with the $70,000 mark. This caution was amplified as both the US and Chinese markets were closed for holidays on Monday. There is no clear indication that Bitcoin is bound for further downside based solely on the negative BTC futures funding rate. However, establishing sustainable bullish momentum will likely depend on a reduction in macroeconomic uncertainty.

data.title: UNITED PARCEL SERVICE INC SEC 10-K Report
data.description:
United Parcel Service Inc. (UPS), a global leader in package delivery and logistics, has released its 2025 Form 10-K report. The report provides a comprehensive overview of the company's financial performance, business operations, strategic initiatives, and the challenges it faces in the current market environment.

Financial Highlights

UPS reported a total revenue of $88,661 million for 2025, marking a 2.6% decrease from the previous year. This decline was primarily attributed to the impact of the Coyote divestiture and planned volume declines. Operating profit stood at $7,867 million, a 7.1% decrease from 2024, influenced by increased pickup and delivery expenses and a shift in international volume to less profitable trade lanes. Net income for the year was $5,572 million, reflecting a 3.6% decrease from 2024, impacted by strategic actions and a complex macro environment. Basic and diluted earnings per share both decreased to $6.56, down by 3.0% and 2.8% respectively, influenced by gains from sale-leaseback transactions and strategic cost management efforts.

Business Highlights

UPS operates a comprehensive global package delivery and logistics network, serving over 200 countries and territories. The company offers a wide range of services including transportation, delivery, distribution, contract logistics, ocean freight, airfreight, customs brokerage, and insurance.

In 2025, UPS focused on growing its market presence in healthcare, business-to-business (B2B), small- and medium-sized businesses (SMBs), and international markets. The company expanded its healthcare logistics capabilities through the acquisitions of Frigo-Trans and Andlauer Healthcare Group (AHG), generating over $11 billion in revenue from its global healthcare portfolio.

The U.S. Domestic Package segment saw a decrease in average daily package volume by 8.6%, primarily due to planned volume declines from its largest customer and revenue quality actions. However, revenue per piece increased by 7.1% due to favorable customer and product mix. The International Package segment experienced a 2.5% increase in average daily package volume, driven by growth in Europe, the Middle East, and Africa (EMEA), with revenue per piece increasing by 1.1%.

Supply Chain Solutions (SCS) revenue decreased by 17.0%, primarily due to the divestiture of the Coyote business and demand softness in air and ocean markets. However, the healthcare logistics business within SCS saw revenue growth due to strategic acquisitions. UPS also undertook significant network reconfiguration and efficiency initiatives, including the closure of 93 facilities and a reduction of 48,000 positions, to enhance operational efficiency and align with strategic volume declines.

Looking ahead, UPS plans to continue its focus on high-quality revenue and margin expansion, with expectations of further cost savings from network reconfiguration initiatives. The company also anticipates opening a new air hub in the Philippines in 2026 and expanding in Hong Kong by 2028 to improve access and transit times in Asia.

Strategic Initiatives

In 2025, UPS executed its Customer First, People Led, and Innovation Driven strategy, focusing on reducing volume from its largest customer by 50% by June 2026, enhancing network efficiency through automation, and expanding its healthcare logistics portfolio with acquisitions of Frigo-Trans and AHG. The Network Reconfiguration and Efficiency Reimagined initiatives aimed to consolidate operations and reduce costs, achieving $3.5 billion in savings. The company also insourced its SurePost product, replacing it with Ground Saver to improve operational control.

UPS returned $6.4 billion to shareholders through $1.0 billion in share repurchases and $5.4 billion in dividends. It issued $4.2 billion in fixed-rate and floating-rate senior notes and repaid $2.1 billion in senior notes. The company maintained a disciplined capital allocation strategy, with $5.9 billion in cash and marketable securities, and reduced capital expenditures to $3.7 billion, focusing on network enhancements and technology initiatives.

Looking forward, UPS expects $3 billion in savings from the Network Reconfiguration and Efficiency Reimagined initiatives in 2026. It anticipates capital expenditures of approximately $3.0 billion in 2026, with 80% allocated to network and technology projects. The company aims to optimize its capital structure through debt refinancing and expects favorable impacts from the One Big Beautiful Bill Act on its effective tax rate and cash flows.

Challenges and Risks

UPS operates in over 200 countries and territories, exposing it to various economic, political, and social developments that could materially adversely affect its operations. The company is subject to numerous laws and regulations, including those related to aviation, transportation, environmental, security, labor, and data protection, which could increase operating costs or result in substantial fines if not complied with.

Key risks include economic and geopolitical uncertainties, competitive pressures, changes in customer relationships, labor disputes, cybersecurity threats, and climate change impacts. The strategy to reduce volume from Amazon poses a risk if not managed effectively. Additionally, compliance with complex and stringent laws, including those related to climate change and data protection, could increase operating costs.

Management is focused on executing the Customer First, People Led, Innovation Driven strategy, which includes reducing volume from Amazon and enhancing network efficiency through automation. The company is also investing in healthcare logistics and expanding its international operations to mitigate risks associated with changing trade policies and economic conditions.

UPS is exposed to market risks such as fluctuations in fuel and energy prices, foreign currency exchange rates, and interest rates. The company uses hedging strategies to mitigate these risks, but there is no assurance of their effectiveness. Changes in these rates could materially affect UPS's financial condition.

SEC Filing: UNITED PARCEL SERVICE INC [ UPS ] - 10-K - Feb. 17, 2026

data.title: OSHKOSH CORP SEC 10-K Report
data.description:
Oshkosh Corporation, a leading manufacturer of specialty vehicles and vehicle bodies, has released its 2025 Form 10-K report, detailing the company's financial performance, business operations, strategic initiatives, and the challenges it faces. The report provides a comprehensive overview of Oshkosh's activities across its main segments: Access, Vocational, and Transport, and outlines the company's future outlook and strategic direction.

Financial Highlights

Oshkosh Corporation reported the following key financial metrics for 2025:

  • Net sales: $10,422.3 million, reflecting a decrease of 2.9% from 2024 due to lower organic sales volume in the Access and Transport segments, partially offset by higher sales in the Vocational segment and improved pricing.
  • Gross income: $1,819.0 million, representing a gross margin of 17.5%, down from 18.4% in 2024, primarily due to higher labor and overhead costs.
  • Operating income: $939.5 million, a decrease of 7.0% from 2024, driven by lower gross margin associated with lower sales volume and higher costs, offset by improved pricing and lower intangible asset impairments.
  • Net income: $647.0 million, down from $681.4 million in 2024, impacted by lower operating income and a decrease in the provision for income taxes.
  • Diluted EPS: $10.02, reflecting the impact of share repurchases and lower net income compared to 2024.

Business Highlights

Oshkosh Corporation's business performance in 2025 included the following highlights:

  • Revenue Segments: The company operates through three main segments: Access, Vocational, and Transport. The Access segment focuses on aerial work platforms and telehandlers, the Vocational segment includes custom and commercial firefighting vehicles, aviation ground support products, and refuse collection vehicles, while the Transport segment is centered on tactical vehicles and delivery vehicles for the USPS.
  • Geographical Performance: Approximately 20% of Oshkosh's net sales in 2025 were derived from sales to the U.S. government, primarily under multi-year defense contracts. The company also has a significant international presence, with sales and operations across various global markets.
  • Sales Units: The Access segment experienced a decrease in sales volume due to softer market conditions and the expiration of a production agreement. The Vocational segment saw increased production rates, contributing to higher sales volumes, while the Transport segment's sales were impacted by the completion of a major contract.
  • New Product Launches: Oshkosh introduced several new products, including the JLG AG619 agriculture telehandler, the Oshkosh AeroTech Tempest-si Deicer, the JLG Micro-Sized Scissor Lift, the McNeilus Volterra ZSL and ZFL electric refuse vehicles, and the Pierce Volterra electric fire truck.
  • New Production Launches: The company invested in new facilities in South Carolina and Tennessee, enhancing its production capabilities and supporting future growth.
  • Future Outlook: Oshkosh expects consolidated sales to reach approximately $11.0 billion in 2026, with significant growth anticipated in the Vocational and Transport segments. The company plans to continue focusing on innovation, customer service, and expanding its product portfolio to drive future success.

Strategic Initiatives

Oshkosh Corporation's strategic initiatives and capital management activities in 2025 included:

  • Financial Flexibility: The company entered into a $500 million unsecured term loan in March 2025, which was used to reduce borrowings under its Revolving Credit Facility.
  • Share Repurchase Program: Oshkosh continued its share repurchase program, buying back nearly 2.3 million shares for $278 million.
  • Dividend Increase: The company announced an 11.8% increase in its quarterly dividend rate to $0.57 per share, marking the twelfth consecutive year of double-digit percentage increases.
  • Capital Management: Oshkosh maintained a stable capital structure with a debt to total capitalization ratio of 19.5% as of December 31, 2025. It had $479.8 million in cash and cash equivalents and $1.51 billion of unused capacity under its Revolving Credit Facility.
  • Future Outlook: The company expects consolidated sales to reach approximately $11.0 billion in 2026, with a focus on increasing production volumes and improving pricing strategies. Oshkosh plans to spend $200 million on capital expenditures in 2026, prioritizing capacity and strategic investments. The company projects cash flow from operations to be between $750 million and $850 million in 2026, supporting its ongoing strategic initiatives and capital management activities.

Challenges and Risks

Oshkosh Corporation faces several key risks and challenges, including:

  • Market Risks: The cyclical nature of the access equipment market, influenced by economic conditions, construction spending, and regulatory changes, poses a significant risk. A downturn in these markets could adversely affect sales and profitability.
  • Operational Risks: The company’s performance under the USPS contract is uncertain, with risks including potential order reductions, production delays, and higher-than-expected warranty costs. Additionally, the company faces challenges in expanding manufacturing capacity, which may take longer or cost more than anticipated.
  • Regulatory Risks: Changes in trade policies, such as increased tariffs, could disrupt supply chains and increase costs. The company also faces risks related to compliance with environmental laws and potential climate change regulations, which could lead to increased operational costs.
  • Supply Chain Risks: Dependence on third-party suppliers exposes the company to risks of supply shortages and price increases, which could disrupt production and increase costs.
  • Financial Risks: Fluctuations in raw material prices and foreign exchange rates could adversely impact financial results. The company also faces risks related to changes in tax rates and potential additional tax liabilities.
  • Cybersecurity Risks: Increasing cybersecurity threats pose a risk to the company’s systems and data integrity, potentially leading to operational disruptions and financial losses.

Management highlights the importance of strategic acquisitions and investments in technology to drive growth and mitigate risks. The company is focused on expanding its product portfolio and enhancing its technological capabilities, particularly in electrification and autonomy, to address changing market demands and regulatory pressures.

SEC Filing: OSHKOSH CORP [ OSK ] - 10-K - Feb. 17, 2026

data.title: Monte dei Paschi plans to take full control and delist Mediobanca
data.description:
Monte dei Paschi di Siena (MPS) will take full control of Mediobanca and delist it while preserving its brand, ending weeks of uncertainty over the future of the merchant bank.

MPS, which was bailed out by the state in 2017 and reprivatised in 2023-2024, last year acquired 86% of Mediobanca, raising question marks over whether it made sense to leave Mediobanca listed with such reduced floating capital.

The plans had created division within the company, with directors led by representatives of investor Francesco Gaetano Caltagirone opposing full integration of Mediobanca.

In presenting full-year results last week, MPS Chief Executive Luigi Lovaglio said the decision whether to delist Mediobanca was pending but MPS was working to maximise savings from the tie-up.

MPS said on Tuesday that the board had decided to proceed with the delisting, without clarifying how the bank will acquire the 14% stake it does not already own.

It will then merge Mediobanca into the group and set up an unlisted entity to house its private and investment banking activities under the Mediobanca brand.

The new entity will also hold the 13% stake that makes Mediobanca the largest investor in Italian insurer Generali .

MPS is due to unveil a strategic plan for the combined group on February 27.

data.title: Uber and Life360 Expand Partnership to Include Linked Accounts
data.description:
By Katherine Hamilton

Uber Technologies and Life360 expanded their partnership to allow users of the location-sharing app to link their accounts with Uber.

The companies said Tuesday that this will make it easier for parents and teens to coordinate rides and stay informed in real time.

Uber and Life360's collaboration already had features to help family members coordinate, such as giving users ride reminders when members landed at airports.

The new integration will be available later this year.

Uber launched a special teen accounts feature in 2023, which lets parents invite their teens to create a specialized account that parents can supervise.

Write to Katherine Hamilton at katherine.hamilton@wsj.com

data.title: Law firm says crypto miner's board waged 'scorched earth’ campaign to block legal fee
data.description:
By Mike Scarcella

U.S. law firm Lehotsky Keller Cohn has sued a committee of cryptocurrency miner Rhodium Enterprises’ board and an investor group, accusing them of interfering with an $11 million legal fee and attacking the firm’s reputation amid Rhodium’s bankruptcy.

Lehotsky Keller filed the lawsuit in U.S. bankruptcy court in Houston on Friday, alleging that a special board committee and investors orchestrated a “scorched-earth” effort to delay and reduce payment of a success fee that the firm said it earned helping Rhodium reach a $185 million settlement last year with Whinstone, a unit of cryptocurrency mining company Riot Platforms .

Texas-based Lehotsky Keller, a small firm founded in 2021 by a pair of former U.S. Supreme Court law clerks, had represented Rhodium before it sought bankruptcy protection in 2024. The firm was later retained in the bankruptcy as special litigation counsel to Rhodium.

Lehotsky Keller in its lawsuit said Rhodium’s special board committee, which was set up to steer the company through bankruptcy, refused to negotiate with the law firm over the size of its stake in the Whinstone settlement, and instead set out to impede the fee.

Lehotsky Keller said in the lawsuit that it suffered “severe” harm and has spent more than $1.5 million in fees and expenses “defending against defendants’ baseless attacks and recovering its success fee.”

The law firm declined to comment. Rhodium, an attorney for the special committee, and an attorney for the investors' group, did not immediately respond to requests for comment.

Lehotsky Keller claims the special committee refused to share information, ignored a requirement to negotiate the Whinstone settlement fees in good faith and made accusations in public that Lehotsky Keller breached its client duties and committed malpractice.

The special committee accused the firm of improperly trying to secure its success fee before the allocation of the Whinstone settlement had been determined, the lawsuit said. Lehotsky Keller denied any wrongdoing and said in court filings that the accusations against the firm were subsequently withdrawn.

A judge last December approved Lehotsky Keller's success fee. The special committee and investors' group have appealed the order.

The case is Lehotsky Keller Cohn v. Special Committee of the Board of Directors of Rhodium, U.S. District Court, Southern District of Texas, No. 24-90448.

For Lehotsky Keller: Joshua Wolfshohl and Michael Dearman of Porter Hedges

For Rhodium: no appearances yet

data.title: Measles cases in South Carolina rise by 12 to 962, state health department says
data.description:
South Carolina reported a surge to 962 measles cases on Tuesday, state health data showed, including 12 additional infections since Friday, as officials warned the widening outbreak could last weeks or months amid lagging vaccine uptake.

The outbreak, which began in October, has been centered in the northwest part of the state, which includes Greenville and Spartanburg, according to the South Carolina Department of Public Health.

The South Carolina outbreak is part of the nation's largest measles surge in more than three decades, federal health data show.

Of those infected, 893 were unvaccinated, 20 were partially vaccinated with one of the recommended two-dose measles-mumps-rubella vaccines, 26 were fully vaccinated and 23 had unknown vaccination status.

There are currently 127 people in quarantine and eight in isolation, the state health department said.

Last week, U.S. Food and Drug Administration Commissioner Martin Makary, in an interview, urged Americans to get vaccinated for measles.

"I think everyone in this administration has been pretty clear that the best way to prevent measles is to get your kid vaccinated against measles," he said.

data.title: Germany Natural Gas Futures Fall to 5-Week Low
data.description:
German natural gas futures fell below €32 per megawatt-hour, the lowest in five weeks, as milder weather forecasts reduced heating demand and eased near term supply concerns.

Warmer and windier conditions are expected across central and western Europe, with temperatures in Germany projected to rise slightly above seasonal averages.

Despite the price decline, storage levels remain tight, with inventories across the European Union below 34%, the lowest since 2022, and German storage under 24%.

However, ample LNG supply and lower US prices have improved availability, helping limit fears of immediate shortages.

Natural gas prices in the United States also fell to a four month low on warmer weather, boosting export potential to Europe.

data.title: Norwegian Cruise Has Slipped Behind Rivals — Market Talk
data.description: Norwegian Cruise Line is getting interest from activist investors as it falls behind rivals in terms of specialty offerings, Truist analysts say. The cruise line hasn't opened a private island — though it plans to do so this summer — while Royal Caribbean is already planning to open its second island next year and Carnival opened one last summer, the analysts note. Private-island stops have become an important sales driver for cruises, typically bringing a 20% ticket pricing premium compared with cruise itineraries without island stops, the analysts say. Norwegian also lacks a competing product to Royal Caribbean's mega-ships, which have proven extremely popular among customers, the analysts say. (katherine.hamilton@wsj.com)

data.title: Automotive Properties REIT Announces February 2026 Distribution
data.description:
/NOT FOR DISTRIBUTION TO U.S. NEWS WIRE SERVICES OR DISSEMINATION IN THE UNITED STATES/

TORONTO, Feb. 17, 2026 /CNW/ - Automotive Properties Real Estate Investment Trust (the "REIT") today announced a cash distribution of $0.0685 per REIT unit for the month of February 2026, representing $0.822 per REIT unit on an annualized basis. Payment will be made on March 16, 2026 to unitholders of record as at February 27, 2026.

About Automotive Properties REIT

Automotive Properties REIT is an unincorporated, open-ended real estate investment trust focused on owning and acquiring primarily income-producing automotive and other OEM dealership and service properties located in Canada and the United States. The REIT's portfolio currently consists of 91 income-producing commercial properties, representing approximately 3.4 million square feet of gross leasable area, in metropolitan markets across British Columbia, Alberta, Saskatchewan, Manitoba, Ontario and Québec in Canada, and Florida and Ohio in the United States. Automotive Properties REIT is the only public vehicle in Canada focused on consolidating automotive and OEM dealership and service real estate properties. For more information, please visit: www.automotivepropertiesreit.ca.

SOURCE Automotive Properties Real Estate Investment Trust

/CONTACT:

Copyright CNW Group 2026

data.title: Hedge Fund Jane Street Raised BlackRock’s IBIT Holdings To $790 Million
data.description:
Key points:
  • Jane Street increased its IBIT holdings by 54% quarter over quarter, adding more than 7.1 million shares in the fourth quarter of 2025.
  • The firm’s total position rose to 20.3 million shares, valued at about $790 million, making it the fourth-largest shareholder of the ETF.
  • Bitcoin fell nearly $2,000 during U.S. trading hours on Tuesday, while retail sentiment around both IBIT and BTC remained in ‘bearish’ territory.

Jane Street Group has raised its stake in BlackRock’s iShares Bitcoin Trust (IBIT) during the fourth quarter of 2025, marking an increase of 54% from last quarter. 

According to a regulatory filing, the firm’s fourth-quarter 2025 13F showed that Jane Street bought 7.1 million additional IBIT shares during the quarter. The purchase raised its total holdings to 20.3 million shares valued at roughly $790 million at the end of December last year. 

The firm bought 7,105,206 shares of BlackRock’s iShares Bitcoin Trust in the fourth quarter of 2025, worth about $276 million. It now holds 20,315,780 IBIT shares valued at roughly $790 million. Before the new data dropped, Jane Street held 13.21 million IBIT shares. This makes Jane Street the fourth-largest shareholder of IBIT. 

iShares Bitcoin Trust ETF (IBIT) traded at $37.99, down by 0.9% in the past 24 hours. On Stocktwits, the retail sentiment around IBIT remained at ‘bearish’ territory, with chatter at ‘low’ levels over the past day.

Sharp Move In BTC Price

Jane Street’s indirect exposure to Bitcoin (BTC) comes as the cryptocurrency saw a sharp price move earlier Tuesday, falling by nearly $2,000 within minutes. Bitcoin has struggled to find a clear direction since the forced sell-offs two weeks ago, when many traders who had borrowed money lost everything. According to Wintermute, an algorithmic trading firm, trading in the regular market slowed, but positions in the futures markets drove prices sharply in both directions. Short-term leveraged trades drove most of the daily volatility, but long-term investors didn't buy enough to sustain it.

How Did Stocktwits Users React?

Bitcoin (BTC) traded at $67,856.03, up by 0.4% in the past 24 hours. On Stocktwits, the retail sentiment around Bitcoin remained in ‘bearish’ territory, with chatter at ‘low’ levels over the past day.

data.title: Home Depot, Lowe's Seen Benefiting from AI Adoption — Market Talk
data.description: Home Depot and Lowe's should see more positives than negatives from the rise of artificial intelligence, in part because home improvement retailers are less dependent on drivers like retail media to support their overall profitability, UBS analysts say in a note. As a result, they won't face a big drag if traffic is diverted away from their websites by agentic AI, they say. Additionally, home improvement tends to be a considered and complicated purchase, meaning the adoption of agentic commerce will likely lag behind in this sector, they say. AI technology should also be deflationary, which should support hard asset prices, like home values, in the long run, benefiting home improvement retail, they say.(kelly.cloonan@wsj.com)

data.title: U.S. 1-year bills high rate 3.345%
data.description:
WASHINGTON, Feb 17 (Reuters) - The U.S. Treasury Department said its Dutch bidding auction of 1-year bills brought these results:

1-YEAR BILLS

RATES

High 3.345% Median 3.300% Low 3.250% Investment 3.480%

PRICE/ACCEPTANCES

Price 96.617833 Accepted at high 32.64% Bid-to-cover ratio 2.96

AMOUNTS TENDERED AND ACCEPTED (dollars)

Total accepted 50,000,067,700 Total public bids tendered 147,854,387,700 Competitive bids accepted 48,883,470,000 Noncompetitive bids accepted 1,111,597,700 Fed add-ons 2,284,255,300

BILLS DETAILS

Issued date Feb 19, 2026 Maturity date Feb 18, 2027 CUSIP number 912797TV9

Dutch auctions are also called uniform-price auctions.Successful bidders pay only the price of the lowest acceptedbid, rather than the actual price they bid as in amultiple-price auction.

data.title: U.S. 6-week bills high rate 3.640%
data.description:
WASHINGTON, Feb 17 (Reuters) - The U.S. Treasury Department said its Dutch bidding auction of 6-week bills brought these results:

6-WEEK BILLS

RATES

High 3.640% Median 3.620% Low 3.530% Investment 3.706%

PRICE/ACCEPTANCES

Price 99.575333 Accepted at high 35.75% Bid-to-cover ratio 2.75

AMOUNTS TENDERED AND ACCEPTED (dollars)

Total accepted 90,000,391,200 Total public bids tendered 247,564,623,700 Competitive bids accepted 88,580,117,500 Noncompetitive bids accepted 1,420,273,700 Fed add-ons 4,111,659,800

BILLS DETAILS

Issued date Feb 19, 2026 Maturity date April 02, 2026 CUSIP number 912797SD0

Dutch auctions are also called uniform-price auctions.Successful bidders pay only the price of the lowest acceptedbid, rather than the actual price they bid as in amultiple-price auction.
